methods.description: Process for preparing rock samples and analyzing them for 10Be content to study glaciation timing.

steps.description: Samples were crushed and sieved to keep the grain size between 200 and 500 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and sieve rock samples to specified grain size

steps.description: The magnetic fraction was isolated using a Frantz magnetic separator.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate magnetic fraction using Frantz separator

steps.description: Quartz was isolated from the other minerals by selective dissolution with several etchings by a mixture of H2SiF6 and HCl.
Quartz grains were etched three times with concentrated hydrofluoric acid (HF) to remove the atmospheric 10Be fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: etch quartz grains with HF to remove atmospheric 10Be

steps.description: Approximately 0.5 g of the Be standard solution was used to spike the quartz samples.
steps.searchable_summary: spike quartz samples with Be standard solution

steps.description: The 10Be target was prepared following the protocol of Brown et al. (1991), modified by Merchel and Herpers (1999).
After total dissolution by concentrated HF, 10Be was extracted using chromatography techniques with anionic and cationic exchange resins.
Before conditioning for the AMS analysis, the Be target was oxidized to beryllium oxide (BeO) by heating to 900°C.
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize Be target to BeO by heating

steps.description: The beryllium ratio (10Be/9Be) was measured using accelerator mass spectrometry at the ASTER AMS facility.
The 10Be/9Be ratio was normalized with in-house CEREGE standard reference material.

methods.description: Collection and analysis of chronological data using radiocarbon and OSL techniques to date glacier advances.

steps.description: Available ages obtained from radiocarbon and OSL techniques were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect radiocarbon and OSL age data

steps.description: Radiocarbon ages were calibrated into ka Cal BP with the IntCal 20 calibration curve.

methods.method_name: Sampling Strategy for Cosmogenic Nuclide Analyses
methods.description: Strategy for selecting and sampling boulders for cosmogenic nuclide 10Be exposure dating to determine glaciation timing.

steps.description: Nine glacial boulders and 12 erratic boulders were described and sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: describe and sample glacial and erratic boulders

steps.description: The biggest, well-anchored, and quartz-rich boulders were chosen to minimize potential remobilization.
A hammer, chisel, and angle grinder were used to sample rock chips measuring 4 cm thick.
steps.searchable_summary: use tools to sample rock chips

steps.description: Sampling targeted the top of the boulders to minimize the effect of snow and/or sediment burial.
steps.searchable_summary: target top of boulders for sampling

steps.description: Topographic shielding values were measured using a clinometer and a compass.
